description: §19.5 `?` SUCCESS unwrap (RT): inner('ok') returns the id (no fail); outer's `inner(id)?` unwraps the success value and binds it to `v`; outer returns it; the `!{}` handler's arms never fire and `r` = 'ok'; the handler lifts @phase = Phase.Ok('ok'). Proves `?` unwraps the success case (not just the error case).
rationale: §19.5.2 step 1: on a success value, `?` unwraps it and produces the success value. So outer('ok') -> 'ok' through the `?` frame, r = 'ok', @phase lands Phase.Ok('ok'). E-ERROR-* silent (`?` in a `!` fn is legal; handled call).
